clusters.root_cause_summary: The goal floor receives insufficient exploration coverage because the agent either burns 87–93% of its step budget on the starting floor before stair entry (p53SfW6mjZe), exhausts goal-floor frontiers after only ~47 floor_steps and re-descends (mL8ThkuaVTM), or triggers a premature non-stair floor switch while already within 1.1m of the goal (q3zU7Yy5E5s). In all three cases the exploration clock on the goal floor expires before detection.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: the goal is on a floor the agent departs before detecting it; DP1 and DP2 changes had zero measurable effect across all three scenes; all currently score 0–25% SR. Different: p53SfW6mjZe physically climbs stairs but not until step 394–439 (380+ floor-0 steps consumed first); mL8ThkuaVTM climbs successfully at step 113 but floor-2 frontiers exhaust at floor_step≈47 triggering a redundant downstair re-descent; q3zU7Yy5E5s never crosses stairs — a non-stair floor switch fires at floor_step ~63 while the agent is 1.1m from the couch, meaning the failure can occur even when stairs are not the transition mechanism.
clusters.transfer_notes: The candidate_2 disconnection watchdog worsened both mL8ThkuaVTM (PointNav thrash, stair centroid never reached) and q3zU7Yy5E5s (spurious forced floor switches, DTG 1.1m→3.9m), confirming that generic watchdog patches designed for navmesh-disconnected scenes are actively harmful to this cluster. p53SfW6mjZe and mL8ThkuaVTM together show that insufficient stair-approach priority (starting too late) and insufficient post-arrival coverage (frontier exhaustion too fast) are independent failure modes — a DP12 recalibration alone will not cover both.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: A hard floor-steps exploration ceiling on any single floor before forcing stair evaluation (preventing the 380-step floor-0 overstay in p53SfW6mjZe and the floor_step≈47 early exhaustion in mL8ThkuaVTM), combined with a Mss-proximity veto that blocks non-stair floor switches when the best active frontier is within 2m and Mss exceeds a minimum threshold — directly blocking the premature switch in q3zU7Yy5E5s where the agent was 1.1m from the couch at switch time.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The agent issues STOP on a visually similar but incorrect object — a white pillar or trash can mistaken for the TV in mv2HUxq3B53 (DTG ~9.9m, never near actual goal), and a non-toilet object at step 110 in qyAac8rV8Zk — because the detection confidence gate before STOP is too permissive to discriminate the goal class from distractors at exploration range.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: both scenes carry an explicit 'false_positive' log classification; stair traversal and frontier scoring are not the failure mode; neither scene was affected by DP1, DP9, or navmesh-watchdog patches. Different: mv2HUxq3B53 is a pure false_positive with 0% SR and STOP called at floor_step=0 immediately after reinit; qyAac8rV8Zk has a separate TV goal instance that already succeeds at 100% SR across all candidates — only the toilet goal instance fails, and it fires mid-episode at step 110, indicating the confidence gap is object-class-specific (toilet vs TV-lookalike objects).
clusters.transfer_notes: qyAac8rV8Zk's TV success with zero false positives across all candidates confirms the STOP gate is calibrated well for TV but undershoots for toilet and visually confusable objects. A universal confidence threshold lift risks over-suppressing valid detections on easy cases; a per-class or DTG-proximity-gated threshold (STOP is only legal when DTG < X meters to detected centroid) would prevent the mv2HUxq3B53 call at DTG ~9.9m without degrading qyAac8rV8Zk/TV.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: STOP_detection_confidence_threshold combined with a DTG proximity gate — require both a minimum BLIP-2/VLM confidence score AND that the agent is within a maximum distance (e.g., 2.5m) of the detected object centroid before STOP is legal, preventing the DTG ~9.9m false call in mv2HUxq3B53 and the step-110 false call in qyAac8rV8Zk without suppressing the correct TV detections that already succeed.

clusters.root_cause_summary: T6_STAIR_CLIMB_EVAL reaches SUCCESS (in_stair_map=False, reach_centroid=True at paused_step=3) but floor_reinit() is never called on the SUCCESS branch in _process_stair_climb_state; floor_step increments 293→294 instead of resetting to 0, anchoring the agent to the pre-climb floor map for the remaining 23 steps — insufficient to locate the bed.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. The floor_step=293→294 post-SUCCESS transition is direct code-level evidence that the SUCCESS branch in _process_stair_climb_state omits a floor_reinit() call. Two prior paused_step resets (10→0, 7→0) before the final successful exit at paused_step=3 indicate the agent correctly exited and re-entered the stair polygon on three separate attempts — yet no reinit fired on any of them, ruling out a one-off race condition and confirming a structural omission in the SUCCESS code path.
clusters.transfer_notes: Both candidate_1 and candidate_2 are behavioral no-ops (identical fingerprints), meaning DP1 rescaling and the navmesh watchdog never reached the actual bug. The fix is isolated to _process_stair_climb_state and is independent of all frontier-scoring and watchdog levers. If this SUCCESS-branch omission exists in all code paths that invoke T6_STAIR_CLIMB_EVAL, fixing it here transfers directly to any scene where the agent physically exits the stair polygon but fails to reinitialize floor state — making the audit scope broader than bxsVRursffK alone.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: map_controller.py:_process_stair_climb_state SUCCESS branch — add floor_reinit() call and floor_step reset to 0 immediately upon SUCCESS state transition; additionally verify climb_direction=2 semantics to confirm the reinit targets the correct destination floor rather than re-initializing the origin floor.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The T6_CENTROID_BYPASS patch fires on a scene with finite geodesic distance (candidate_1 proves natural stair traversal by step ~83), triggering 22 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid: False failures and burning 152 steps before the bypass forces Phase 2 at paused=8 — a patch regression that delays the floor switch from step ~83 to step 235 and leaves only 264 steps to find the couch on floor 2.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. The bypass was designed for navmesh-disconnected scenes (geodesic=inf) but fires on DYehNKdT76V because paused_count accumulates from stair approach geometry mismatch rather than true disconnection. The 22 Reach_stair_centroid: False entries are a consequence of the patch holding Phase 2 behind the paused=8 gate, not underlying navmesh geometry — candidate_1's natural traversal at step ~83 directly refutes disconnection.
clusters.transfer_notes: The same T6_CENTROID_BYPASS logic is also the fix target for genuinely disconnected scenes. Gating the bypass on a geodesic_distance==inf pre-check would restore candidate_1 behavior for DYehNKdT76V while preserving bypass functionality for scenes where the centroid is truly unreachable. The anomalous -1.000-score frontiers appearing in candidate_2 after step 235 (absent from candidate_1) suggest a secondary side-effect of the delayed bypass on frontier state that warrants investigation before deploying any paused threshold reduction.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: Gate T6_CENTROID_BYPASS on geodesic_distance==inf as a pre-check — the watchdog should be a no-op when geodesic_distance is finite, restoring natural stair traversal by step ~83 as demonstrated in candidate_1. A secondary option is reducing the paused threshold from 8 to 3–4 to recover ~100 of the 152 wasted steps if the geodesic gate is not immediately feasible.

clusters.root_cause_summary: The agent correctly identifies the toilet via VLM (visible at frames ~27 and ~69), enters navigate mode at step 72, and issues STOP at step 77, but receives an 'unknown_failure' evaluation tag — a class absent from the standard glossary — indicating STOP was called with the goal object confirmed in bbox but with the agent positioned outside the evaluation success radius, a repeatable geometry mismatch across all 4 candidates.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. All four candidates produce an identical mode sequence (fingerprint-identical), identical step count (77), and identical 'unknown_failure' tag. No stair traversal, no LLM invocations (dp7_empty=0/0), zero DP1/DP2/DP12 influence. The failure is entirely within the navigate-mode final approach distance — the agent halts slightly outside the evaluation success radius despite the object being visually confirmed.
clusters.transfer_notes: Candidate_1 (DP1 reduction) and candidate_2 (navmesh watchdog) had zero effect, confirming the failure is at the STOP evaluation boundary rather than any upstream navigation parameter. The 'unknown_failure' classification is distinct from 'false_positive' (object not detected) and 'bad_stop_true_positive' (position clearly wrong) — it likely encodes a BBox-overlap-but-not-centroid-proximity condition. Any fix must either reduce the approach-halt distance in navigate mode to physically enter the success radius or audit the evaluation boundary to determine whether the success radius threshold is misaligned with the navigate-mode STOP trigger distance.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: navigate_mode_approach_distance_budget — reduce the distance at which the agent issues STOP in navigate mode so it physically enters the evaluation success radius before stopping; alternatively, audit the 'unknown_failure' evaluation boundary condition to determine if it encodes BBox-match-without-centroid-proximity and patch the STOP trigger to add a centroid proximity check matching the evaluation success radius.

clusters.root_cause_summary: After a valid two-floor stair traversal completing by step ~92, the post-switch floor offers only one usable frontier (Mss=0.107) while the second is permanently suppressed (raw=-1.000), causing a 407-step oscillation loop as the agent approaches to 2.0m but cannot resolve the frontier near a local navmesh boundary; the goal couch was visible on the starting floor at step 0 but no descent-back mechanism exists after ascent.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. The 407-step distance oscillation (2.0m→3.2m→2.0m) on a single frontier coordinate is a navmesh-boundary stall: the frontier is navigably close but the target cell is unreachable at the sub-2m scale. The second frontier's permanent -1.000 raw score leaves no escape alternative. The couch on the starting floor was present at step 0; candidate_1's later stair entry (step 64, 17 explore steps) vs candidate_2's step 47 (7 explore steps) both failed to detect it before ascending, confirming that delaying stair entry alone cannot fix this scene without a return mechanism.
clusters.transfer_notes: The geodesic-inf watchdog (candidate_2) never fired, ruling out navmesh disconnection and confirming this is a frontier-quality and descent-logic failure on the post-switch floor. candidate_1's earlier stair entry exploration parameter change (17 vs 7 floor-0 steps) produced no SR improvement, demonstrating that the missing lever is post-switch escape (descent back or frontier reachability pre-filter), not pre-switch exploration depth. A navmesh reachability pre-filter that removes unresolvable frontiers before commitment would prevent the 407-step waste as an independent fix even without descent logic.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: Post-floor-switch descent logic — when all post-switch frontiers are exhausted or all remaining frontiers score Mss below a minimum threshold without detecting the goal, re-enter stair mode to descend to the previous floor and resume coverage; paired with a navmesh reachability pre-filter that removes frontiers the agent can approach to <2m but cannot resolve, eliminating the 407-step oscillation stall even before descent logic is available.

forbidden_moves.reason: Globally mandatory forbidden move per system constraint; explicitly ruled out in every individual scene analysis in the database.

Mss coefficient reduction (1.0→0.8) had zero observable effect in p53SfW6mjZe, mL8ThkuaVTM, and q3zU7Yy5E5s — the failure is floor-switch timing and exploration budget allocation, not frontier scoring order; explicitly ruled out in all three scenes.
LLM frontier-selection trigger changes do not affect when the agent switches floors or how many floor_steps are allocated per floor; ruled out in mL8ThkuaVTM and q3zU7Yy5E5s.

The watchdog actively worsened both mL8ThkuaVTM (PointNav thrash, stair centroid never reached in candidate_2) and q3zU7Yy5E5s (spurious forced floor switches, scene-avg DTG 1.1m→3.9m); none of the three scenes have navmesh disconnection.
Frontier scoring cannot trigger the missing floor_reinit() call in _process_stair_climb_state; the bug is a code-path omission in state machine logic, entirely upstream of any scoring parameter.
reach_centroid=True throughout all T6 entries in bxsVRursffK; the watchdog never triggers and is structurally irrelevant to the missing reinit on the SUCCESS branch.

candidate_1 proves finite geodesic for DYehNKdT76V via natural stair traversal at step ~83; the 22 Reach_stair_centroid: False entries are caused by the T6_CENTROID_BYPASS patch holding Phase 2 behind the paused=8 gate, not by a disconnected navmesh component.
Floor-switch interval tuning does not prevent the agent from calling STOP on a wrong object; explicitly ruled out in both mv2HUxq3B53 and qyAac8rV8Zk.

mv2HUxq3B53 already switches floors successfully via frontier-exhaustion path; qyAac8rV8Zk TV goal already succeeds via normal stair traversal. The false_positive failure is in post-navigation STOP detection confidence, not stair mechanics.
Stair carrot waypoint distance has no bearing on STOP detection confidence or object discrimination; neither mv2HUxq3B53 nor the qyAac8rV8Zk toilet instance involves a stair traversal failure.
TEEsavR23oF is a single-floor episode with zero frontier scoring influence on the STOP outcome; the failure is purely a navigate-mode approach distance vs evaluation success radius geometry mismatch.
TEEsavR23oF involves zero floor switches (stair_mode_runs=0, floor_reinits=1 is initial setup only); floor-switch interval is structurally inapplicable.

The watchdog never fired in XB4GS9ShBRE candidate_2, confirming the post-switch 407-step oscillation stall is a frontier-quality and descent-logic failure, not navmesh disconnection; applying the watchdog would be a no-op at best.
